title: Hanna Ding
content:

{{Quote|Well done, Scout. You proved you can beat up little boys, as long as you are allowed to cheat. How proud you must be.|Hanna Ding, to Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy|<ref name="YDR" />}}
'''Hanna Ding''' was an [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who studied at the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Temple]] [[Jedi academy|academy]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Ding underwent the [[Initiate Trials/Legends|trials]] to become a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] and passed them by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Ding participated in the [[Jedi Temple Apprentice Tournament]], organized by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ilena Xan]], and managed to advance to the second round. She faced [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiate]] [[Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy]] in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]] in the dark, and despite her displaying superior skill, Enwandung-Esterhazy won the fight through the unorthodox means of grabbing Ding's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] blade and forcing her to submit with a right-[[Limb/Legends|handed]] choke-hold.

==Biography==
===Jedi training===
[[File:JediTemple-Deceived.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Jedi Temple where Hanna Ding trained]]
Hanna Ding was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]]<ref name="YDR" /> [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]].<ref name="HannaAge" /> She became a member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and lived at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. During her [[Time/Legends|time]] at the [[Jedi academy|academy]], she underwent [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]<ref name="YDR" /> and later [[Initiate Trials/Legends|graduated]] to become a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] to a [[Hanna Ding's Jedi Master|Jedi Master]].<ref name="TCSWE" />

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> when the pan-[[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|erupted]] between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Ding was stationed at the [[Jedi temple/Legends|Temple]]. By this time, she had constructed a [[Color/Legends|green]]-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Ding frequently engaged in practice [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|fights]], during which [[Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy]], a [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiate]], observed her for any weaknesses.<ref name="YDR" />

===The Apprentice Tournament===
{{Quote|Come now, Esterhazy. Aren't you going to try one of your dirty tricks on me? You didn't use them all up on poor little Pax, did you?|Hanna Ding, to Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy|<ref name="YDR" />}}
[[File:Jedi_Training_Droid.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Combat Training Chamber where the Apprentince Tournament was held]]
In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="HannaAge" /> Ding participated in the [[Jedi Temple Apprentice Tournament]], which was administrated by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Ilena Xan]] at the Temple's [[Jedi Temple sparring arena|Combat Training Chamber]]. Ding openly criticized Enwandung-Esterhazy, claiming that she used unconventional trickery and manipulated the rules when she beat Initiate [[Pax Chizzik]]. Ding attempted to get Enwandung-Esterhazy disqualified from the tournament, but Chizzik rose to Enwandung-Esterhazy's defense by taking the blame for forgetting the rules.<ref name="YDR" />

In the second round of the Tournament, Ding was pitted against Enwandung-Esterhazy. As a surprise stipulation, they were instructed to fight in complete darkness with [[Training lightsaber/Legends|training lightsabers]] to simulate realistic [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]] conditions. When Xan signaled the start of the duel, Ding launched a high [[Force jump/Legends|leap]] attack from above, but Enwandung-Esterhazy skillfully parried the attack, having observed Ding perform the same opening maneuver in her previous fights. Ding then chased after her opponent with a series of slashes, but Enwandung-Esterhazy responded slyly by fleeing to the spectators in order to escape and even used Xan as a human shield to block Ding's lightsaber. Changing tactics, Ding channeled the Force to slow down Enwandung-Esterhazy's [[Sword/Legends|sword]] [[Limb/Legends|hand]], allowing her to strike her opponent's thigh with the first of three required cuts to win the match and [[Telekinesis/Legends|disarm]] Enwandung-Esterhazy of her [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]].<ref name="YDR" />

[[File:Tallisibeth_Enwandung-Esterhazy.jpg|thumb|right|210px|Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy, Hanna Ding's tournament opponent]]
Ding demanded that she yield, but Enwandung-Esterhazy refused. Unwilling to attack an unarmed foe, Ding failed to anticipate Enwandung-Esterhazy's next move. She seized the blade of Ding's lightsaber with her right hand and used her left to execute a precise choke-hold on Ding's neck. About to lose consciousness, Ding was forced to surrender after seven [[Standard second|seconds]] and was eliminated from the competition. After Enwandung-Esterhazy emerged victorious in the tournament by besting the formidable [[Whie Malreaux]], Ding was part of the spectators and criticized Enwandung-Esterhazy's method of relying on Malreaux's good nature in order to win, continuing by warning Enwandung-Esterhazy's tricks would not work as well on a live combat situation and would get her [[Death/Legends|killed]].<ref name="YDR" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Vindictive stuck-up Arkanian prig. She's just mad because you beat her, fair and square.|[[Lena Missa]] on Hanna Ding, to Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy|<ref name="YDR" />}}
Ding had a haughty demeanor typical of Arkanians, often displaying a sense of superiority toward Enwandung-Esterhazy. She was a stickler for official rules and regulations and, on more than one occasion, sought to have Enwandung-Esterhazy disqualified from the Apprentice Tournament. Ding believed that Enwandung-Esterhazy's use of tricks to gain an advantage was a form of cheating.<ref name="YDR" />

Her fastidious nature made the gritty, close-quarters fighting of [[Unarmed combat/Legends|hand-to-hand combat]] unappealing to Ding. She preferred to be standing two paces away from her opponent, allowing her lightsaber to do the fighting for her. Ding's preference for her lightsaber made it her weakness according to Enwandung-Esterhazy.<ref name="YDR" /> The fear of dying in the Clone Wars lingered within Ding.<ref name="TCSWE" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|But…I won. Now you yield.|Hanna Ding to Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy|<ref name="YDR" />}}
Ding honed her [[Force power/Legends|skills]] in utilizing the Force in various ways, including telekinesis and Force jumping. In addition, Ding's lightsaber combat skills were polished, as Enwandung-Esterhazy noted. She described it as a circular style of swordplay with fast slashes that continuously changed angles.<ref name="YDR" />

==Equipment==
Ding owned a lightsaber that emitted a green blade.<ref name="YDR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Hanna Ding first appeared in ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'', a novel written by [[Sean Stewart]] and published in [[2004]].<ref name="YDR" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' {{Ab|Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book and [[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (abridged audiobook)|abridged audiobook]]}}
